name: Razorpay-Style Payment Notification Pipeline
scenario: Build a payment processing pipeline where the payment API responds in <100ms while notifications (email, SMS, webhook) and retry jobs run asynchronously in the background. Daily reconciliation and weekly reports run on a schedule.
description: PaymentService processes and saves payments fast. NotificationPipeline fires 3 async notifications (email, SMS, webhook). RetryJob checks for failed payments every 5 minutes. DailyReconciliationJob runs at 11:59 PM. Custom thread pool isolates notification threads.

core.description: `NotificationPipeline` @Service with 3 @Async methods: `sendEmail(Payment p)`, `sendSms(Payment p)`, `triggerWebhook(Payment p)`. All return `CompletableFuture<Void>`. Each logs the thread name.
`PaymentService.processPayment(PaymentRequest req)` — saves payment, fires all 3 async notifications using allOf (waits for all to start but not finish), returns payment in <200ms.
`PaymentRetryJob` @Component: @Scheduled(fixedDelay=300_000) — loads FAILED payments, retries each. @Scheduled(cron='0 59 23 * * *') for daily reconciliation.
`AsyncConfig` @Configuration with @EnableAsync, @EnableScheduling: custom ThreadPoolTaskExecutor named 'paymentExecutor' with core=3, max=10, prefix='payment-notif-'.
@SpringBootTest test: processPayment returns in <200ms; all 3 notifications fired (verified via thread names logged within 2 seconds of payment creation).
@SpringBootTest test for retry job: create a FAILED payment, call retryJob.retryFailedPayments() directly, assert payment status changes to RETRIED.

bonus.description: Add AsyncUncaughtExceptionHandler: if any notification fails (simulate by throwing in sendEmail when amount > 100000), the error is logged but doesn't affect the payment response.
Add a rate-limited @Async: if webhook is called more than 10 times/minute, skip it and log 'Webhook rate limited'. Use AtomicInteger counter reset by a @Scheduled(fixedRate=60000) job.
Add async parallel data enrichment: before saving payment, fetch exchange rate (async), fraud score (async), and merchant info (async) using CompletableFuture.allOf, then save enriched payment.

data_to_use:
Payment: id, userId, amount, currency, status (CREATED/FAILED/RETRIED)
Notifications: email (2s simulated), SMS (500ms simulated), webhook (1s simulated)
Retry: find FAILED payments, attempt re-processing, set status=RETRIED
Schedules: retry every 5 min (fixedDelay=300000), reconcile at 11:59 PM (cron='0 59 23 * * *')
Thread pool: paymentExecutor, core=3, max=10, queue=50
example_output:
# On POST /api/payments:
[http-nio-8080-exec-1] Payment PAY-001 saved
[http-nio-8080-exec-1] Returning 201 in 47ms
[payment-notif-1] Sending email for PAY-001...
[payment-notif-2] Sending SMS for PAY-001...
[payment-notif-3] Triggering webhook for PAY-001...
[payment-notif-1] Email sent for PAY-001 (2000ms later)

# Every 5 minutes:
[scheduling-1] Checking for FAILED payments...
[scheduling-1] Found 0 failed payments

# At 11:59 PM:
[scheduling-1] Starting daily reconciliation for 2024-03-15
[scheduling-1] Reconciliation complete: 1523 payments processed

# Tests:
✓ processPayment_returnsIn200ms
✓ retryJob_failedPayment_changesStatusToRetried
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Performance test: `long start = System.currentTimeMillis(); service.processPayment(req); assertTrue(System.currentTimeMillis() - start < 200)`. Verify notifications ran: Thread.sleep(2500) then check log/counter. Self-invocation anti-pattern: fire notifications via injected NotificationPipeline bean, not `this`.
submission_instructions: Push to GitHub. Show logs demonstrating async threads (http-nio vs payment-notif). All tests must pass.
tips_for_success: All 3 notifications: fire all three, don't wait — user gets response immediately
Performance test: store System.currentTimeMillis() before and after processPayment()
Retry job test: inject @Autowired PaymentRetryJob; call method directly
concepts_reinforced: @Async with CompletableFuture<Void> for fire-and-forget with result handle
